zVSome more special functions which may be useful for multivariate statistical
analysis.
gammaln
multigammalnc
Returns the log of multivariate gamma, also sometimes called the
    generalized gamma.
    Parameters
    ----------
    a : ndarray
        The multivariate gamma is computed for each item of `a`.
    d : int
        The dimension of the space of integration.
    Returns
    -------
    res : ndarray
        The values of the log multivariate gamma at the given points `a`.
    Notes
    -----
    The formal definition of the multivariate gamma of dimension d for a real
    `a` is
    .. math::
        \Gamma_d(a) = \int_{A>0} e^{-tr(A)} |A|^{a - (d+1)/2} dA
    with the condition :math:`a > (d-1)/2`, and :math:`A > 0` being the set of
    all the positive definite matrices of dimension `d`.  Note that `a` is a
    scalar: the integrand only is multivariate, the argument is not (the
    function is defined over a subset of the real set).
    This can be proven to be equal to the much friendlier equation
    .. math::
        \Gamma_d(a) = \pi^{d(d-1)/4} \prod_{i=1}^{d} \Gamma(a - (i-1)/2).
    References
    ----------
    R. J. Muirhead, Aspects of multivariate statistical theory (Wiley Series in
    probability and mathematical statistics).
    z*d should be a positive integer (dimension)g
z+condition a (%f) > 0.5 * (d-1) (%f) not metg
?/usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/scipy/special/spfun_stats.py
<listcomp>\

axis)

floor
ValueError
logZ
loggam
range)

<module>
